use_cases: Enterprise AED program deployment across locations
Connected AED response system for faster cardiac arrest response
AED lifecycle management and compliance tracking
Workplace safety and OSHA compliance for cardiac emergencies
Replacing outdated AED fleets with connected, modern devices
pain_points: AED programs are unmanaged — devices expire without anyone knowing
No visibility into AED readiness across distributed locations
Compliance gaps in workplace cardiac emergency preparedness
Legacy AEDs lack connectivity — no alerts, no tracking, no data
Cardiac arrest response times too slow due to AED location confusion
buying_triggers: Workplace cardiac event or near-miss incident
OSHA safety audit or compliance review
New EHS Director or VP of Safety hired
AED fleet approaching end-of-life / mass replacement cycle
Competitor or peer company deploying connected AED programs
Expansion to new facilities requiring AED coverage
